message:
ci(release): attest SHA256SUMS + per-archive .sha256 sidecars (ft-s6ohz)

The attest@v4.1.0 step added in 325054be covered dist/*.tar.xz and
dist/*.zip — the binary archives — but not the SHA256SUMS combined
manifest or the per-asset *.sha256 sidecars produced by the Unix and
Windows checksum steps (release.yml:89, :98-99, :136).

Gap: a downloader who follows the standard "fetch SHA256SUMS, verify,
then download binary" pattern trusts SHA256SUMS to name the right
archive. An attacker with release-step write access (but no attestation
signing key) could rewrite SHA256SUMS to misname entries while the
original .tar.xz attestations stay valid, and gh attestation verify on
the archive alone would still pass.

Fix: extend subject-path to cover every file a downloader might trust.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-1p752","title":"[testing-fuzzing] crash-detector fuzz target for frankenterm-escape-parser Parser::parse raw bytes","description":"# Motivation

`frankenterm_escape_parser::Parser::parse(&[u8], callback)` is the
state machine that consumes untrusted terminal output for every
captured pane. The crate has 2 existing fuzz targets:

  * `termwiz_csi_parser.rs` — structure-aware around CSI sequences
  * `osc_marker_parser.rs`  — structure-aware around OSC markers

Neither is a plain raw-bytes crash detector. State-machine paths
that the structure-aware generators don't reach (SOS/PM/APC,
partial DCS/Sixel streams, intermediate-byte boundary transitions,
pathological ESC chains) go uncovered.

# Scope

New fuzz target `fuzz/fuzz_targets/escape_parser_raw.rs` that feeds
arbitrary bytes into `Parser::parse` with a no-op callback, asserts
no panic / no infinite loop / no allocator blow-up. Size-bounded at
64 KiB to keep iteration latency low (matches the existing raw-bytes
targets in this repo).

# Invariants (beyond crash-freedom)

1. `parse` consumes all bytes it's fed (state machine never strands).
2. The callback is invoked at most some finite number of times per
   input byte (linear, not quadratic).
3. `parse_as_vec` round-trips `parse` — calling both on the same
   input produces identical Action counts.

# Seed corpus

Empty + minimal ESC + malformed-CSI + OSC-with-nulls + APC + nested
DCS partial stream. 4-5 seeds.

# Build

    cargo +nightly fuzz run escape_parser_raw -- -runs=100000

Targets 2000+ exec/s given escape parsing is hot-path byte-driven

 {"id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","title":"Publish user-facing release/tuning guidance for different hardware tiers","description":"Provide clear guidance on expected behavior, config knobs, and troubleshooting paths across low/mid/high-end hardware.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Publish hardware-tier guidance backed by validated unit/integration/e2e/perf evidence from the resize/reflow program.
2. Guidance must include explicit operational playbooks, rollback/fallback steps, and diagnostics workflows for each tier.
3. Require structured logging/diagnostics artifact references so operators can map symptoms to recommended actions quickly.
4. Validate guidance via scripted end-to-end operator journeys and update docs when discrepancies are discovered.
5. Added cross-link from docs/resize-rollout-plan-wa-1u90p.8.2.md so rollout documentation chain now references this guidance.","created_at":"2026-02-20T01:55:29Z"},{"id":2624,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","author":"CobaltCanyon","text":"CobaltCanyon starting a bounded docs/ops slice. +{"id":"ft-1xkh9","title":"[patterns] builtin:core default pack is an empty placeholder","description":"File: crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:1442
Current state: builtin_core_pack() is documented as the shared/core built-in pack but returns PatternPack::new("builtin:core", "0.1.0", Vec::new()).
Impact: config/environment surfaces default to builtin:core, so config-driven PatternEngine construction can silently load zero default detection rules.
Should replace: builtin:core should materialize the shipped default rule set (at minimum the current built-in Codex/Claude Code/Gemini/WezTerm rules) rather than exposing an empty placeholder pack.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:20.454161Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:26.771753Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-26738","title":"restart_scheduler: clamp negative hours_since_last in recency_penalty (LOW)","description":"Fixed in d327d7c1. recency_penalty returned negative values when hours_since_last was negative (clock regression via NTP / VM rewind / manual date reset), propagating through score_window and corrupting SchedulingDecision.windows ranking. Benign in production (negative scores always below min_score_threshold so no trigger fires) but breaks decision-surface invariants. Fix clamps to 0 (treat as just restarted). 2 regression tests pin the invariant. Found during subsystem bug sweep 2026-04-21 (workflows/policy/safety/release-gates, pane6 review).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:27.355801Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:30.892645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:30.892323Z","close_reason":"Fixed in d327d7c1","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-26k3h","title":"[MEDIUM] survival::ActivityProfile::update_hour NaN-poisons hourly_ewma via clamp","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/survival.rs:455` — `ActivityProfile::update_hour`.

Same `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` gotcha as ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5 / ft-x50ce. A NaN `normalized_activity`:

1. `sample = normalized_activity.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` stays NaN.
2. First sample (sample_count == 0) path writes `self.hourly_ewma[index] = NaN`.
3. Any subsequent EWMA update is `alpha * NaN + (1 - alpha) * prev = NaN`.
4. Every `predict_hour` / `predict` call returns NaN for that hour slot, forever.
5. Downstream survival/hazard math using the predicted activity gets NaN — same silent-wrong-answer class as disk_pressure.

## Fix

Guard at entry: if `normalized_activity.is_nan()`, skip the update entirely. The slot keeps its last good value (or the default if uninitialised) and the next well-formed sample continues the EWMA.

## Tests

* `ft_26k3h_update_hour_nan_does_not_poison_ewma` — update_hour(NaN) leaves hourly_ewma + sample_count unchanged.
* `ft_26k3h_update_hour_nan_then_clean_sample_still_tracks` — end-to-end: NaN → clean sample sequence still produces a finite, sensible EWMA value.

## Scope

Single file (`survival.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:50:37.797018Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:53:29.312035Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:53:29.311735Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-2guux","title":"reality-check gaps","description":"Reality-check sweep of the repo (pane 5, session 2026-04-20) against
README.md + AGENTS.md + docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md. Most
capability claims match the code; the four material gaps below are
worth tracking. No fixes this tick — documentation-only finding.

Methodology: read the README "Supported Surface Matrix" and "Quick
Example", then grep the CLI command tree at `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`,
the MCP tools at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs`, the web
endpoints at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/router.rs`, and the
runtime layer at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs` +
`runtime_compat.rs`.

Findings (severity ranked):

G1 [MEDIUM] Watch-daemon asupersync claim vs actual reactor dependency
  Evidence:
    - README.md:38-41 "runtime model is asupersync-native",
      "runtime_compat remains as an audited boundary".
    - crates/frankenterm-core/tests/runtime_labruntime.rs:13-18
      explicit comment:
        "Full ObservationRuntime integration tests (startup,
         shutdown, discovery, capture) require the runtime_compat::task
         module to be migrated from tokio::spawn to asupersync::spawn.
         Until that migration completes, ObservationRuntime::start()
         cannot run under asupersync RuntimeFixture because task::spawn
         → tokio::spawn panics without a tokio reactor."
    - runtime.rs:184 uses `crate::runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx`
      which still goes through the tokio-backed path when the
      `asupersync-runtime` feature is disabled.
  Why it matters: the primary operator surface (`ft watch`) cannot be
  exercised end-to-end under a pure asupersync runtime fixture today.
  The README is honest that runtime_compat is "an audited boundary";
  the specific gap is that the watch daemon's task spawns transit
  that boundary. Tracked by the ft-xbnl0.2.x epic but worth surfacing
  as a doc clarification on README.md:38-41 ("native with a named
  quarantine").

G2 [MEDIUM] `ft restart` is Unix-only, not disclosed in the support matrix
  Evidence:
    - README.md:124 "Session persistence | Supported with backend
      prerequisite | Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and
      `ft session doctor` are implemented".
    - crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27032 emits:
        "ft restart is currently supported only on Unix platforms."
      and exits 1 on non-Unix; the full `execute_restart_workflow`
      call path sits inside `#[cfg(unix)]` at line 27036.
  Why it matters: a Windows operator following the README's
  session-persistence story will hit this only at runtime.
  Supportable via an asterisk in the matrix or an explicit
  "Unix-only" caveat in the `ft restart` section.

G3 [LOW] Performance claims lack linked artifacts
  Evidence:
    - README.md:51 "<50ms lag" for delta extraction.
    - README.md:62 "keeps 200+ panes under 1GB vs 4GB+".
    - README.md:113 "TOON reduces token consumption by 40-60%".
    - No matching benchmark artifact path in docs/ referenced from
      these claims; the verification-contract doc at docs/ft-xbnl0-
      verification-contract.md mandates Level C artifact bundles for
      soak/perf-adjacent claims but the README does not cite any.
  Why it matters: performance claims presented as current fact in
  the README should either cite an artifact bundle or be softened to
  "target" / "design goal" until evidence lands. Non-blocking.

G4 [LOW] `SEND_OSC_SEGMENT_LIMIT = 200` silently bounds OSC133 state
  Evidence:
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:188
        `const SEND_OSC_SEGMENT_LIMIT: usize = 200;`
    - mcp.rs:288-310 `derive_osc_state_from_storage` walks only the
      last 200 segments. If OSC133 markers span further back, the
      derived state is silently incomplete; the helper returns
      `Ok(None)` with no gap marker.
  Why it matters: a quiet-pane edge case (no activity for >200
  segments) would yield an incorrect alt-screen / prompt state via
  the MCP surface without a visible warning. Worth either documenting
  the bound or recording a `markers_unknown_due_to_window_limit`
  signal.

Non-gaps verified present and matching claims:

  - `ft robot state` / `state --include-text`: main.rs:2375-2391
  - `ft robot search --mode {lexical,semantic,hybrid}`:
      main.rs:4633-4711 enum + From impl; semantic/hybrid properly
      gated behind `semantic-search`/`frankensearch` features.
  - `ft mcp serve`: main.rs:33124
  - `ft web` + `/stream/events` + `/stream/deltas`:
      web/router.rs:65,70 — streaming exists; README's
      "more than 'health only'" claim is accurate.
  - `ft distributed agent`: main.rs:165-168 under
      `#[cfg(feature = "distributed")]`.
  - `ft session doctor`, snapshot save/restore/list/inspect/diff:
      SnapshotCommands + SessionCommands enums defined around
      main.rs:4207.
  - `ft tx run`/`tx rollback`/`tx plan`/`tx show`: main.rs:21114+
      via `execute_tx_run_with_executor`.
  - Policy "21 subsystems": unified_telemetry.rs:242 comment; actual
      PolicyEngine struct at policy.rs:3466-3517 declares ~22
      subsystem-shaped fields. Close-enough; no drift worth fixing.
  - tokio/smol usage in supported core: grep finds zero production
      call sites outside `runtime_compat` + `dependency_eradication`
      + `forbidden_dep_guards` (guard files are intentional).

Documented only. No shippable fix this tick per /reality-check-for-project
ground rules. Related: AGENTS.md rule zero (NEVER delete
frankenterm-core) is intact; the four gaps above are additions to the
README, not subtractions from the code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:01:41.726765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:59.915672Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:59.913552Z","close_reason":"Reopening-in-error by p5-orch robot-triage pick — the bead was already closed with the four reality-check gaps (G1 runtime_compat quarantine language, G2 ft restart Unix-only caveat, G3 softened perf/token claims, G4 OSC/OSC133 marker window bound) addressed across two commits:

1. 4c3952a9 docs: correct README claims vs actual (ft-2guux) — landed 2026-04-20, covers all four gaps:
   - G1: added 'The remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses a named runtime_compat quarantine while the last task-spawn seams are being collapsed' to the Platform Model paragraph
   - G2: appended 'ft restart is currently Unix-only.' to the Session persistence row of the support matrix
   - G3: softened <50ms lag to 'target/benchmark lane'; softened 200+ pane memory envelope to 'benchmark-dependent until linked artifacts are published'; softened 40-60% TOON savings to 'lower-token machine format; savings vary by payload'
   - G4: added a new subsection '5.1 Marker Window Bound' documenting the bounded OSC/OSC133 marker derivation window

2. af925d26 docs(readme): correct runtime_compat boundary claim — landed 2026-04-21, further refined G1 language now that the 9e1bd63b cfg-strip has collapsed the dual-runtime fallback: 'The dual-runtime fallback is gone, but several production seams still route through the named runtime_compat boundary for runtime bootstrap, channels, time, blocking work, IPC, and mux interoperability while the remaining compat helpers are being collapsed.'

bv --robot-triage surfaced this bead as open-and-ready because the triage graph snapshot had not yet absorbed the prior close. Verified via git log --grep ft-2guux + direct README diff inspection: no work remains. Restoring closed status.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","docs","readme","reality-check"]}

 {"id":"ft-48xy0","title":"storage.rs: remove dangling single-member blocks left by dual-runtime strip","description":"After ft-xbnl0.2.5 (9e1bd63b) stripped all #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")] guards, the wrappers survived as empty curly-brace blocks around single return statements. Examples:

crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:10543-10548
    {
        let cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);
        return self.create_reservation_with_cx(&cx, ...).await;
    }
crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:10588-10591 (release_reservation)
crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs: ~60+ similar occurrences across all cx-routed entry points.

Each site has the form:
    pub async fn foo(...) -> Result<T> {
        {
            let cx = ...;
            return self.foo_with_cx(...).await;
        }
    }

Not a bug. Cosmetic cleanup. Makes storage.rs 60+ lines lighter and removes the misleading impression that the inner block is a conditional scope. A single sweep with ast-grep or sed would clean it up and the linter/fmt would leave nothing behind.

Low priority — file pristinely tracked so a future /simplify pass does not re-wave over the block boundaries and miss these.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:16:46.113101Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:24.258101Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:24.257850Z","close_reason":"Collapsed 166 dangling cx blocks, stripped 40 redundant returns, removed residual asupersync-runtime cfg gates across 27 files. -504 net lines. cargo check clean. Commit fdd1e817.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4f1fv","title":"workflows handlers test build misses CompatRuntime import for Runtime::block_on","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:03.547663Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:14.479058Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:14.478891Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4o5mb","title":"[conformance] wa.search / wa.get_text / wa.send / wa.wait_for lack schema+envelope golden roundtrip","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance.rs` pins envelope + schema conformance for exactly ONE tool: `wa.rules_list` (tests at lines 237-294, 313-346). `conformance_mcp_manifest.rs` pins the MANIFEST (tool schemas, additionalProperties=false, resource templates) but does NOT exercise the per-tool request → response envelope roundtrip.

`grep -oE '"wa\.[a-z_]+"' crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance.rs | sort -u` → only `"wa.rules_list"`.

Out of 29 advertised `wa.*` tools in `tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`, 28 have zero call-and-assert-envelope coverage in the conformance suite. The four core operator-facing tools are:

* `wa.search` — JSON envelope shape, hit schema, TOON parity, pane_filter/since_filter/until_filter/mode/metrics fields
* `wa.get_text` — pane_text map shape (ok/error variants), truncated + truncation_info edges
* `wa.send` — sender/response envelope (distinct from get_text), error codes for denied sends
* `wa.wait_for` — matched/elapsed_ms/polls/is_regex fields, timeout response envelope, rule_id match path

## Shape of fix

Mirror the `mcp_conformance_rules_list_*` test pattern (lines 237-294 in mcp_conformance.rs):

1. spawn_client() → call_tool("wa.search", …)
2. parse_tool_envelope() → assert_success_envelope_shape() → assert_*_data_matches_documented_schema()
3. Also pin: invalid-format rejection, invalid-args hint text, TOON parity where applicable.

Existing helpers (`assert_object_matches_documented_schema`, `assert_common_envelope_fields`, `assert_success_envelope_shape`, `assert_invalid_args_envelope_shape`) already generalise — the new tests should reuse them verbatim.

## Why this matters

The MCP manifest conformance is the FREEZE on the advertised schema. Without per-tool envelope tests, a refactor can pass `conformance_mcp_manifest` (schema still looks right) while silently returning a non-conforming response body — e.g. field renamed in `ScannerHit` → clients break but tests stay green. ft-bs0ec froze this for `agent_inventory`; the four core operator tools deserve the same treatment.

## Scope guard

One new test file `mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs` (or additions to `mcp_conformance.rs`) with 4× (success + invalid-args) = 8 tests. ~250 LOC. No production changes.

## Explicitly out of scope

Mission / tx / events / cass families (separate bead if needed). Manifest tests (already covered). Resource templates (already covered).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:57.026144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:46:38.216319Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3608,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb","author":"jemanuel","text":"Blocker (pane3, 2026-04-23): fastmcp-rust enforces tool input_schema type validation at the JSON-RPC boundary — wrong-typed required fields (e.g. pane_id string where integer required) return McpError::InvalidParams from call_tool BEFORE reaching the per-tool handler's FT-MCP-0001 envelope path. The pattern mcp_conformance.rs:280 uses for wa.rules_list (optional string agent_type) cannot be replayed for wa.search/get_text/send/wait_for via wrong-typed-required inputs. Next-hop approach: pin the SUCCESS envelope shape via {timeout_secs:1, pane_id:999, pattern:'never-matches', format:'json'} for wait_for (times out in ~1s, returns Ok envelope with matched:false/elapsed_ms/polls). Draft tests left UNTRACKED at crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs — first pass covers invalid-args envelopes and doesn't work as noted. Bead stays in_progress for handoff.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:27:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-4qom2","title":"[SEV] tmux cmd_queue cap only enforced at pop site; unbounded growth when state != Idle","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue has CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000 enforced only inside send_next_command at line 289 via cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286-288 when state != Idle, so the drain-oldest logic never runs if tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse.

Four push sites append to cmd_queue with NO cap check:
- line 196: Event::LayoutChange -> ListAllPanes
- line 223: Event::SessionChanged -> ListCommands
- line 234: Event::WindowAdd -> ListAllWindows
- line 375: split_pane (user split) -> SplitPane

Concrete interleaving: (1) tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse; (2) tmux server is slow/hung (real-world scenario: remote tmux on lagging SSH link, tmux server overloaded, Event::Guarded response delayed); (3) GUI user resizes window repeatedly -> stream of Event::LayoutChange -> each push_back at line 196 runs without cap check; (4) send_next_command early-returns because state != Idle. Queue grows unbounded -> memory leak, eventual OOM.

The CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH comment at line 32-33 explicitly advertises 'prevent unbounded memory growth during event storms' as a safety invariant. That invariant is currently only upheld at the pop site, where it can't run under the exact condition (stuck tmux) that event storms exploit.

Fix: extract the cap-enforcement logic into a push_command_capped helper that applies the drain-oldest + warn/error logging at every push site. The helper runs under the cmd_queue lock (just acquired by the caller), so it's atomic with the push and has no additional locking cost.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:17:26.601099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.160715Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.158111Z","close_reason":"Completed: added push_command_capped() helper and routed all 4 production push sites (LayoutChange/SessionChanged/WindowAdd/split_pane) through it. The hard cap is now enforced at push time, not only at pop time — closing the unbounded-growth window when tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse. cargo check passes clean. Commit 1055cffc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4z7vh","title":"RobotCommands::Workflow builds PolicyEngine without require_prompt_active/command_gate/rules — bypasses operator safety config","description":"AUTH BYPASS (robot mode mutations, Severity: Medium).

crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs has 10 PolicyEngine::new call sites. 8 of them wire require_prompt_active from config + .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()). Two do NOT, both under RobotCommands::Workflow:

Site 1 (main.rs:19831, RobotCommands::Workflow::Run):

  let policy_engine = PolicyEngine::new(
      config.safety.rate_limit_per_pane,
      config.safety.rate_limit_global,
      false, // Don't require prompt active for robot mode
  )
  .with_tuning(&config.tuning);

No command_gate, no policy_rules, and require_prompt_active hardcoded false. This engine is fed into CxPolicyInjector → WorkflowRunner; any workflow step that calls send_text (e.g., handle_compaction sending '/compact', handle_usage_limit sending '/refresh') bypasses:
  - the operator's safety.require_prompt_active=true policy,
  - every [[safety.rules]] custom gate,
  - the [safety.command_gate] allow/deny list.

Site 2 (main.rs:20511, RobotCommands::Workflow::Abort): identical construction. abort_execution_with_cx runs compensation steps through the same injector; any send_text in a compensation path bypasses the same three surfaces.

Compare the correctly-gated paths:
  - RobotCommands::Send at main.rs:17652 uses config.safety.require_prompt_active AND .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules.
  - Non-robot workflow invocations at main.rs:21361, 21533, 25051, 27347 all wire the full policy surface.

NOT a duplicate of ft-48ah6 (read-only policy): this bead is specifically about the write/mutation surface (workflow's send_text / command-bearing steps). ft-48ah6 covered the read/search side.

Fix: replace the hardcoded 'false' with config.safety.require_prompt_active at both sites, and chain .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()) before passing the engine to PolicyGatedInjector. Rate-limit args are already correct.

Regression test: a send inside a robot-invoked workflow must be DENIED when config.safety.require_prompt_active=true AND no prompt is active, matching the direct-send gate behavior. Currently it is allowed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:46:59.925741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084786Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084480Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 834b59f2. Both RobotCommands::Workflow::Run (main.rs:19831) and ::Abort (main.rs:20520) now build PolicyEngine with config.safety.require_prompt_active and chain .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules — matching the construction at RobotCommands::Send (main.rs:17652) and non-robot workflow sites (21361/21533/25051/27347). Regression test robot_workflow_policy_engine_denies_send_when_prompt_required_ft_4z7vh asserts SendText is denied when require_prompt_active=true + command_running. Test binary compile is currently blocked by pre-existing concurrent-agent breakage (E0603 macro select private, E0063 missing defer_fts_triggers field) unrelated to this fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-fs42k","title":"connector_outbound_bridge: dedup uses event.timestamp_ms (parallel ft-dijpe miss)","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs:1027\` passes
\`event.timestamp_ms\` (caller-supplied via the public
\`OutboundEvent::with_timestamp_ms\` builder, line 284) to
\`check_and_record\` as the dedup eviction clock — the exact shape that
ft-dijpe just fixed in the inbound bridge.

The ft-dijpe defect description even claimed *"Outbound bridge doesn't
have this shape — it uses its own \`now_ms\` from \`SystemTime::now()\`
for all internal state."* That claim is wrong. The outbound bridge's
constructor (line 245) does use \`SystemTime::now()\` to seed
\`timestamp_ms\` by default, but:

1. The public \`with_timestamp_ms\` builder (line 284) lets any caller
   override that default to an arbitrary value.
2. \`route_event\` line 1027 then passes that caller-controlled value
   directly to \`OutboundDeduplicator::check_and_record\`, which calls
   \`evict_expired(now_ms)\` and pops anything whose
   \`now_ms - inserted_at_ms > ttl_ms\`.
3. Same replay-bypass attack as ft-dijpe: a caller submitting two
   copies of the same correlation_id with the second copy's
   \`timestamp_ms = ttl_ms + 1\` triggers eviction of the first entry
   and the replay is treated as new.

## Concrete failure path

1. Internal caller (workflow handler, retry loop, test harness) builds
   an \`OutboundEvent\` and calls \`with_timestamp_ms\` to override the
   default \`SystemTime::now()\` (legitimate reason: replaying historical
   events for backfill, fixture-driven tests, or a buggy clock-advance
   helper).
2. First dispatch with \`correlation_id=X, timestamp_ms=0\`.
3. Second dispatch with \`correlation_id=X, timestamp_ms=ttl_ms+1\`.
4. \`evict_expired\` sees \`now_ms=ttl+1 > inserted_at=0+ttl\` → pops X.
5. \`check_and_record\` finds cache empty, inserts a new X, returns
   \`true\` → second dispatch routes as if new. All downstream
   side-effects fire twice (slack post, JIRA ticket, GitHub PR
   comment, credential broker call, etc.).

Trust model is weaker than inbound's — outbound events are
internally constructed rather than attacker-supplied — but the
defense-in-depth argument applies. A buggy internal caller that
mis-sets \`timestamp_ms\` should not silently bypass dedup, and
symmetry with the inbound bridge's hardening makes the codebase
easier to reason about.

## Severity rationale

- Same shape as ft-dijpe (silent dedup bypass), one severity level
  lower because the trust model is internal-only.
- Reachable today via any internal caller misusing
  \`with_timestamp_ms\`. Future caller bugs are likelier than
  attacker reach.

## Fix sketch

Mirror the ft-dijpe fix: in \`route_event\` line 1027, compute
\`now_ms\` from \`SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).as_millis()\`
and pass that to \`check_and_record\` instead of \`event.timestamp_ms\`.
The \`event.timestamp_ms\` field stays useful as an analytics signal
(end-to-end source→sink latency, dispatch-history correlation) but
must NOT determine when dedup entries expire.

## Regression test

\`bridge_dedup_ignores_event_timestamp_ms_for_eviction\`: dispatch X
with \`correlation_id='r', timestamp_ms=0\`, then dispatch X with
\`correlation_id='r', timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2\`. Assert the second is
\`deduplicated=true\`. Pre-fix the second returns \`deduplicated=false\`
because evict_expired with the spoofed future-timestamp pops the live
entry.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs\` only.
The line-1080 \`let now_ms = event.timestamp_ms\` (used downstream for
\`created_at_ms\`, \`broker_checked_at_ms\`, \`dispatched_at_ms\`) is a
separate concern — those telemetry timestamps arguably should also
be SystemTime::now() but that's a wider change. This bead covers
ONLY the dedup eviction clock.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:25:28.629996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:19.140282Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:19.139925Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-ptjlp (same defect, same line, parallel filing race). Sibling agent already applied the SystemTime::now() fix in working tree at connector_outbound_bridge.rs:1037-1044 with their own ft-ptjlp tag in the inline comment. Closing this bead so ft-ptjlp owns the fix and the test work. Reviewed their fix — it's identical to what I would have written (SystemTime::now() → dedup_now_ms → check_and_record), correctly preserves event.timestamp_ms for downstream telemetry uses.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-gc4hz","title":"retry: cancelled operations must not count as circuit-breaker failures","description":"with_retry_and_circuit_cx pattern-matches Err(_) and calls circuit.record_failure() unconditionally. Cx-initiated cancellation (Error::Cancelled) therefore pollutes the circuit breaker's consecutive_failures counter — an external cancel can push the breaker past failure_threshold and reject subsequent unrelated requests with CircuitOpen. Cancelled operations represent caller intent, not backend failure, so they must not affect breaker state. Fix: in with_retry_and_circuit_cx, match Err(Error::Cancelled(_)) and skip both record_success and record_failure (the result is simply propagated).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:13.437155Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.645671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.644374Z","close_reason":"Landed in 503c9506 (retry: exempt Error::Cancelled from circuit-breaker failure count) + 765743d5 (regression test) + 79029eeb (test fix: private fields -> snapshot()).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-s4c6y","title":"retry::RetryPolicy::delay_for_attempt panics on NaN backoff_factor or jitter_percent (no fail-closed)","description":"RetryPolicy::delay_for_attempt computes base_ms = initial_ms as f64 * backoff_factor.powi(exp); then jitter_range = base_ms * jitter_percent; then rng.random_range(-jitter_range..=jitter_range). If backoff_factor or jitter_percent is NaN (from a malformed config, a fuzz harness, or a direct struct-literal construction bypassing new()), base_ms / jitter_range become NaN, and rng.random_range(NaN..=NaN) panics. Matches the fail-closed pattern already applied to disk_pressure (ft-761tz), continuous_backpressure (ft-yskcu), telemetry::Histogram (ft-b4l62). Fix: sanitize backoff_factor and jitter_percent inside delay_for_attempt — if either is not finite or out-of-range, substitute a safe default and return a non-NaN delay. Add proptest: any RetryPolicy with any f64 in backoff_factor/jitter_percent must produce a finite, non-panicking Duration for any attempt in 0..=63.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:09.137669Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:18.432344Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:18.431974Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-s4myu","title":"storage: foreign_keys PRAGMA not applied on connection open for up-to-date DBs — CASCADE deletes silently skip","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs` `StorageHandle::with_config` (line 6002) opens the writer connection via `Connection::open(&db_path_owned)` and then calls `initialize_schema(&conn)`. The `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON` statement is ONLY present inside `SCHEMA_SQL` (line 104) which is executed via `conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_SQL)` at line 4349/4375.

For an UP-TO-DATE database (user_version == SCHEMA_VERSION, the common case on every frankenterm restart), `initialize_schema` short-circuits at line 4341-4344:

```rust
if current == SCHEMA_VERSION {
    // Already up to date
    ensure_ft_meta(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
    return Ok(());
}
```

...returning WITHOUT executing SCHEMA_SQL. SQLite's `PRAGMA foreign_keys` is PER-CONNECTION — it must be re-issued on every new connection. Since the ONLY place that sets it is the schema-init path, every writer connection on an existing DB has `foreign_keys = OFF` (the SQLite default).

## Impact (HIGH)

Every ON DELETE CASCADE in the schema silently fails to fire. Concrete example:

```rust
fn prune_session_checkpoints_sync(conn, session_id, retention) -> Result<usize> {
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1 AND id NOT IN (...)")
}
```

`mux_pane_state` rows reference `session_checkpoints(id) ON DELETE CASCADE` at schema line 646. On an up-to-date DB, the DELETE removes the checkpoint row but leaves ORPHAN mux_pane_state rows. Over time these accumulate indefinitely.

Same pattern affects 15+ other references in SCHEMA_SQL (output_segments→panes, events→panes, workflow_executions→panes, etc.). Every `delete_session`, retention sweep, and explicit pane cleanup path leaks orphan rows.

Silent data corruption accumulates across restarts. A fresh-DB test suite (which runs SCHEMA_SQL) would never catch this.

## Fix

Apply `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON` unconditionally on every connection open, after `Connection::open` and before the initialize_schema short-circuit. One-liner:

```rust
let conn = Connection::open(&db_path_owned)?;
check_and_recover_wal(&conn, &db_path_owned)?;
conn.pragma_update(None, "foreign_keys", true)
    .map_err(|e| StorageError::Database(format!("Failed to enable foreign keys: {e}")))?;
initialize_schema(&conn)?;
```

This is idempotent (the pragma is a per-connection toggle) and costs O(1) per open.

## Tests

- `prune_session_checkpoints_cascades_mux_pane_state`: open storage on pre-populated DB, call prune with retention=0, assert every mux_pane_state row for deleted checkpoints is gone (vs current behavior where they persist).
- `fresh_db_and_existing_db_both_enforce_foreign_keys`: open on fresh + reopen on existing, in each case attempt to INSERT a mux_pane_state with a non-existent checkpoint_id, assert SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_FOREIGNKEY.

## Severity

HIGH. Silent data corruption, invisible to fresh-DB tests, accumulates across restarts. 15+ FK definitions in SCHEMA_SQL silently no-op for existing databases. Fix is one line.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:35:18.699462Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:44:16.969109Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:44:16.968817Z","close_reason":"Withdrawn — premise was wrong. The bundled SQLite that libsqlite3-sys builds compiles with `-DSQLITE_DEFAULT_FOREIGN_KEYS=1` (libsqlite3-sys-0.36.0/build.rs:127), so every rusqlite Connection::open starts with foreign_keys=1 by default. Empirically confirmed by running the reopen test: conn2 had PRAGMA foreign_keys returning 1 (not 0). The PRAGMA statement inside SCHEMA_SQL is redundant-not-missing. CASCADE deletes DO fire on fresh connections even when initialize_schema short-circuits. Reverted the uncommitted storage.rs edit. Note: if frankenterm ever switched to non-bundled SQLite or moved off libsqlite3-sys, this defense would be needed — but under the current Cargo.toml ('rusqlite = { features = ["bundled", ...] }' at Cargo.toml:246) there is no live defect.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-s6l5b","title":"[testing-fuzzing] pin match_rule_glob invariants under proptest (empty patterns, star-wildcards, length preservation)","description":"# Gap

events.rs:1121 `pub fn match_rule_glob(pattern, value) -> bool` —
a hand-rolled glob matcher with `*` (any sequence) and `?` (any
single char) support. 19 inline example tests at events.rs:2827+ cover
specific cases. No proptest / property-based coverage exists.

# Risk

Hand-rolled glob matchers are classic defect surfaces — backtrack
state machines have edge cases that example tests routinely miss:

1. Empty-pattern vs empty-value semantics (false positives here
   would break exclude rules)
2. Pattern of N `?`s must match exactly N-char values (length-
   preservation invariant that's easy to regress with an off-by-one)
3. `"*"` alone must match every value (including empty) — this is
   the "allow-everything" escape hatch operators rely on
4. Exact patterns (no wildcards) must be length-preserving: only
   match same-length values with same bytes
5. Idempotence: `match_rule_glob(x, x) == true` for any exact pattern x

# Fix

New proptest suite in events.rs test module with 5 invariant properties,
each bearing the domain check the hand-rolled state machine is supposed
to guarantee. Runs under the existing module's test harness — no new
test file or feature-gate.

# Catches

- Backtrack-reset off-by-one at events.rs:1144 (p_star assignment
  wrong-side) would fail invariant #1/#3
- Wildcard-in-early-exit regression at :1122 would fail #4
- Star-at-end (`"a*"`) improperly requiring trailing chars would
  fail #3 on pattern="a??" value="abc"

-{"id":"ft-s6ohz","title":"[review][LOW] release.yml SLSA attestation covers binary archives but not SHA256SUMS manifest","description":"release.yml:147-149 subject-path covers dist/*.tar.xz and dist/*.zip — the binary archives attested by actions/attest@v4 (SLSA build provenance). Not covered: SHA256SUMS combined manifest (created at :137-138 as `cat *.sha256 > SHA256SUMS`) and individual .sha256 sidecar files.

Scenario: release uploads dist/* so downloaders get both ft-linux-amd64.tar.xz + SHA256SUMS. A verifier who trusts SHA256SUMS to key the right .tar.xz and only attests the .tar.xz, never re-checksums. An attacker who compromised the release step (not the attestation step) could re-write SHA256SUMS to misname the manifest entries without breaking the .tar.xz attestation signature.

Practical impact: minimal for verifiers who use gh attestation verify on the .tar.xz directly. But users following the 'download SHA256SUMS, verify, then download binary' pattern get no provenance coverage on the manifest they trust.

Fix: extend subject-path to include SHA256SUMS (and possibly *.sha256 sidecars). Minor YAML edit:

    subject-path: |
      dist/*.tar.xz
      dist/*.zip
      dist/SHA256SUMS

 {"id":"ft-s6sss","title":"[review][LOW] ft version --help example shows --verbose but flag is --full","description":"crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:170-181 defines the Version subcommand with:

    #[command(after_help = r#"EXAMPLES:
        ft version                        Short version line
        ft version --verbose              Detailed build metadata
    ..."#)]
    Version {
        /// Show detailed build metadata (commit, rustc, target, features)
        #[arg(long)]
        full: bool,
    },

The example at line 173 shows 'ft version --verbose' but the actual clap flag is '--full' (line 180). Running 'ft version --verbose' hits clap 'unexpected argument' instead of the verbose build block.

This is pre-existing drift not caused by 86e08660, but 86e08660's README Quickstart change directs first-touch operators at 'ft version --full' — any operator who then runs 'ft --help' or 'ft version --help' will see --verbose documented and try that first, get a clap error, and have to re-discover --full.

Fix: update the example at main.rs:173 to 'ft version --full' so the help text matches the flag. One-line edit.

Severity: LOW — cosmetic, UX friction for first-touch only. Discovered during reviewer audit round 3, 2026-04-23 while verifying the ft-b4mif README fix in 86e08660.

@@ -159,9 +159,16 @@ jobs:
       - name: Generate build provenance attestations
         uses: actions/attest@59d89421af93a897026c735860bf21b6eb4f7b26 # v4.1.0
         with:
+          # Attest every file a downloader might trust: the binary archives AND the
+          # checksum artifacts that key archive verification. Without attesting
+          # SHA256SUMS + *.sha256, an attacker who compromises the release step
+          # (but not the attestation step) could re-point the manifest at a
+          # different archive that still has its original valid attestation.
           subject-path: |
             dist/*.tar.xz
             dist/*.zip
+            dist/*.sha256
+            dist/SHA256SUMS
 
       - uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
         with:
